item.title: movie facts
item.description:
in Joker, Joaquin Phoenix chose this outfit himself to pay homage to his all time favourite movie star; McLovin
In Wonder Woman 1984, Steve is amazed by an escalator despite them being commonly used in stores by the time he died in 1918. This is a reference to how bad the script is
In The Rise of Skywalker, Darth Vader's destroyed mask is frequently displayed. This is a nod to Disney destroying Darth Vader's character arc
In Wonder Woman, Diana is able to get to London from her remote island in only a day because she is an Amazon, and therefore qualifies for next day shipping
Ellen Ripley in the movie Alien (1979) was originally written to be a man. Director Ridley Scott changed his mind when he watched The Hunger Games (2012) and realized that women can also be action movie leads
Actors in black and white movies were often putting their lives in danger during driving scenes, as they weren't able to tell if the traffic light was red or green
in Watchmen a graffiti reads "Who watches the Watchmen?" this is a reference to me, since I watched the Watchmen movie
In Morbius, the main characters uses their wolf form to win an arm wrestling competition for control of New Jersey, this is a reference no one can prove wrong, because no one’s seen this movie
Vin Diesel’s full legal name, Vehicle Identification Number Diesel, was the sole determining factor in his original casting for the Fast & Furious franchise
Despite his name, actor Adam Driver was not consider for a role in Baby Driver. This is because he was 33 when the film came out, meaning he was an Adult Driver
In Thunderbolts, Buckey Barnes is a Congressman. This is because he is 108 years old which is a prime age for being in Congress
In Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle, Dwayne Johnson's avatar did not have the power to read the map. This is because paper beats Rock
item.dc:creator: HonestlyNotARobot

item.title: The first pride was a riot, and it wasn’t all that long ago.
item.dc:creator: blesstherainsdownincastamere

item.title: Top 10 reasons why Americans aren’t protesting
item.description:
Hi! My name is tallroses and I have opinions. They are often unpopular, but I’m autistic and I do a lot of analysis when I form my opinions so I tend to be pretty confident in them. I live in California and I am asked on the internet, nearly daily, why we aren’t just out there dismantling the regime by any means necessary, so I made a handy list for you!
people actually protest quite a bit, but it’s not something that gets covered by the media.
Delaney Hall protests happening literally right now https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/delaney-hall-ice-protests-dhs-restores-family-visitation/
despite the stereotype of the lazy American, we tend to work long hours and feel tremendous pressure not to take time off. Striking doesn’t really hurt employers. The negative effects just heavily fall on our coworkers. Our health care is also often tied to our job and we don’t want to jeopardize that.
strange but true, I’ve been told that the poor reputation of Americans abroad stems from the idea that no one could be this polite without it being fake. Let me assure you, it’s not an act. We really are this polite. We’re forced to smile until it’s not forced anymore. We’re supposed to play nice and not upset anyone. Any declarative statement (like a protest slogan) is going to lead to an etiquette/PC argument instead of achieving its intended purpose. UPDATE since everyone wants to discuss the behavior of tourists around the world. I’m not talking about tourism. This was just a weird example of how Americans can be so polite that we come off as fake. Another example is how my 10 yo daughter wrote an article about what to do if ICE is in your town. It was aggressive and violent. It made everyone uncomfortable. Parents thought it went too far. I posted it here and had to remove my post because people either thought I was lying or that I was a bad parent. Did anyone talk about ICE and the shit they are doing!? No. We debated the “appropriateness” of the protest instead.
we’ve been burned…a lot! We want to get behind causes, then we find out the leaders/organizers are grifters. Groups that seemed to want change turned out to want our money. Everything is a scam. Like # 3, any real purposeful action is scrutinized and there is rarely consensus.
our independent/lone ranger culture means that we usually disengage from something as soon as everyone is doing it. If we change our profile pic to the Ukrainian flag, we feel like we’re making a statement. When everyone changes their profile pic, we feel like sheep. When the pink pussy hats became a thing, we embraced it. We had been waiting for a simple, unifying, symbol. Then we learned that huge groups of protesters and activists felt othered and excluded. We were like, well shit, we screwed up. Now we second guess ourselves at every turn. We don’t want to be sheep following a trend (the wrong trend). It’s quite difficult to unit 300 million people without a symbol they can all get behind and doesn’t piss a huge chunk of them off.
for the past 20-30 years, out of date tactical military equipment has been funneled into local police departments. To defend against some type of invasion? No! To be used against the very citizens they are sworn to protect. And they do! They do use it against us.
you may be aware of the shear quantity of guns owned by private people in the US. The Venn diagram of gun enthusiasts and trump supporters is close to a complete circle. These “peacekeepers” have been known to show up to protests to support police and menace protestors. Interactions have been fatal.
the US is much larger than people realize. We can’t quickly take a train from place to place to participate in a protest in a centralized location/city. Our main form of transportation is driving and it can take days to get to a major city. Even then, if we all tried to descend on DC or NYC, the travel infrastructure would have difficulty accommodating everyone, especially from the West coast. We can’t just “come together”.
in the US, violent protest isn’t called violent protest, it’s called rioting. As I said in # 1 peaceful protest is rarely covered by the media and anything else is not protesting anymore, because it’s not polite. If at any time there is violence or property damage by the protestors, the media will stop calling it a protest and call it a riot. Media coverage of riots is fraught with racism and classism. They might focus on one piece of footage of a small group of people looting a business and pivot the discussion to a rise in crime. Days or weeks of “news” panels will discuss the value of the damages and how could the rioters have thought it was okay to do this. The political message of the protest will not be discussed.
due process and other constitutional rights are being flagrantly dismissed and when we speak up about it to our lawmakers they say, “wow, isn’t that awful”, and then proceed to do absolutely nothing about it. # 3 also means that anyone speaking out about unlawful treatment is put under scrutiny for somehow doing something to deserve said treatment and again the discussion ignores the real issue. Unless our plan is some kind of government overthrow, we need our lawmakers and courts to stand up to these violations.
item.dc:creator: tallroses

item.title: When mowing the lawn, leave a patch of grass for the apex predators to practice hunting
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: Very detailed Peridot I cut.
item.description: This gem was cut by Mike Moriarty (Me!) It is a natural peridot from Pakistan and weighs 7.85 carats. It is an original design with 176 facets ( Apex Symophonia )— it’s a very lively stone with beautiful minty color. Edit: Anyone who is interested or curious about gems or lapidary should join us this Wednesday at 7:05pm CT— we have our monthly live stream where we answer many questions in the chat and show our latest creations like this gem: https://www.youtube.com/live/g4FvCVkV02g?si=Vw9yKDa57toRmMMH
item.dc:creator: MoreGems

item.title: Just sayin'....
item.description: Considering the horrid things Pam Bondi has done, I don't have a shred of sympathy for her. She saw proof of little girls being raped and tortured and she helped cover it up, so maybe her thyroid cancer is just the universe balancing her Karmic checkbook. Either way, fuck Pam Bondi.

26 Man Tax: Doug Sampaio
"Those who apply themselves too closely to little things often become incapable of great things." - La Rochefoucauld So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc. Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, **Imgur Creator Spotlight is now in your hands!! Having trouble finding people I haven't already featured recently. Now I'm going to just do who YOU nominate. Tag me or DM me and I'll feature whoever you pick.**
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.description:
https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/01/us/politics/hegseth-navy-promotion-list.html?smid=url-share
Whitewashing our military and disparaging minorities is all this administration knows how to do. You cannot support the troops and support this administration. You cannot be patriotic and support this administration. They are fascist and plain and simple. If you don't think women can lead, just hit the block button. Also, in case people didn't know, trans women are women and trans men are men.- That concludes this TED Talk.
